messages.key: call for submissions the 25th annual preicis workshop on hci lisbon portugal december 13 2026
messages.subject: Call for Submissions - The 25th Annual Pre-ICIS Workshop on HCI, Lisbon, Portugal (December 13, 2026)
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues, We are pleased to accept submissions to the 25th Annual Pre-ICIS Workshop on HCI Research, to be held in Lisbon, Portugal on December 13, 2026, in conjunction with the International Conference on Information Systems (ICIS 2026). IMPORTANT DATES: Submissions Due: August 31st, 2026 Acceptance Notification: October 11th, 2026 Camera-ready manuscripts due: October 25th, 2026 Workshop event: December 13th, 2026 WORKSHOP FORMAT:  The workshop will feature a keynote speaker, as well as completed research and research-in-progress papers accepted for presentation either in the main program as Oral Presentations or as part of a Rapid Research Roundtable (RRR). The RRR offers an opportunity for less matured papers, which have not been accepted for oral presentations, to gain exposure and benefit from the collaborative feedback of the community. Given the diverse goals of this workshop, a non-exhaustive list of potential topics is provided below: Achieving Digital HCI in the Age of AI Analysis, design, development, evaluation, and usage of information systems Digital collaboration and coexistence in HCI Guidelines and standards for interface design HCI considerations of generative AI Design and evaluation of innovative interaction technologies Design and evaluation of mobile and/or web interfaces Interface design for collaborative or competitive environments Interface design for individual and/or collective usage Interface design for the elderly, the young, and/or other special needs populations Adaptive, context-aware interface design Novel forms of authentication and/or authorization (e.g., mousing or typing dynamics) Impact of interface design on beliefs, attitudes, emotions, behaviors, productivity, and/or performance Cognitive, Affective, and/or Conversational HCI Data analytics in HCI Gamified approaches to HCI Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (AI) Human-Robot Interactions Neuroscientific approaches to HCI Nudging strategies and techniques in HCI Usability engineering (i.e., friction reduction), ease-of-use and user experience Ethical issues in HCI Pedagogical issues related to HCI education Implications and consequences of HCI advances on individuals, collectives, and society FAST-TRACKING OPPORTUNITIES WITH THCI: The best completed research papers from the workshop will be fast-tracked for publication consideration in the AIS Transactions on Human-Computer Interaction (AIS THCI). SUBMISSION, REVIEW, AND ACCEPTANCE PROCESS: The workshop seeks high-quality submissions detailing completed research or research-in-progress. Authors should identify their submission as “Completed Research” or “Research in Progress” on the first page below the title. Submissions will undergo a double-blind review process. The submissions should not have been published or be under review elsewhere. All accepted submissions will be published in the workshop proceedings, which will be made available electronically via the AIS digital library. SUBMISSION FORMAT: All submissions must be formatted for letter size (8.5- x 11-inch where 1 inch = 2.5 cm) and have 1-inch margins on each side. Please use Times New Roman 12-point font with single spacing for the body of the paper. The first page of the manuscript should have a title, the type of the submission (Completed Research or Research-in-Progress), total word count of the submission, an abstract of 150 words or less, and a list of 5-6 keywords. Maximum length for submitted manuscripts are as follows: Completed Research Paper → 14 pages Research-in-Progress → 7 pages These page limits are inclusive of all text, figures, and tables, but do not include the cover page, abstract, keywords, or references. Only submissions in Word format will be accepted for review. Authors are invited to submit their manuscripts on the submission website by the submission deadline communicated above. The submission system will open on August 10th, 2026, and instructions will be provided on www.sighci.org. Note that as this is a cooperative workshop environment, we expect submitting authors to serve as reviewers of other manuscripts. ORGANIZING COMMITTEE: Workshop Co-chairs: Sameh Al Natour, Toronto Metropolitan University (salnatour@torontomu.ca); Chee-Wee Tan, Hong Kong Polytechnic University (chee-wee.tan@polyu.edu.hk) Program Co-chairs: Ryan Schuetzler, Brigham Young University (ryan.schuetzler@byu.edu); Bingqing Xiong, Deakin University (b.xiong@deakin.edu.au) Digital Chair: Dave Wilson, Brigham Young University (davidwilson@byu.edu) On behalf of the 2026 Pre-ICIS HCI Workshop Organizing Committee

messages.key: call for participation 2026 preicis sigdsa symposium lisbon portugal dec 1213 2026
messages.subject: CALL FOR PARTICIPATION — 2026 Pre-ICIS SIGDSA Symposium, Lisbon, Portugal (Dec 12–13, 2026)
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues, We are pleased to announce the 2026 Pre-ICIS SIGDSA Symposium, to be held December 12–13, 2026, in Lisbon, Portugal, in conjunction with the International Conference on Information Systems (ICIS 2026). Aligning with the ICIS 2026 theme, “Analytics and AI for Digital Collaboration and Coexistence,” this year's Symposium theme is: “Analytics and AI-Based Decision Support for Digital Collaboration and Coexistence.” This theme reflects the growing role of analytics, artificial intelligence, and decision support systems in enabling collaboration across digital, organizational, and societal contexts, while addressing the opportunities and challenges of coexistence in an increasingly connected world. The Symposium invites scholars, educators, doctoral students, and practitioners to explore how analytics, AI, machine learning, generative AI, and decision support technologies can contribute to more effective, ethical, inclusive, and sustainable forms of digital collaboration. The Symposium features three tracks: Research Track Teaching and Prototype Track Doctoral Student Workshop Track Important Dates: July 13, 2026 (Mon): Manuscript submissions open August 31, 2026 (Mon): Manuscript submission deadline (11:59 PM EDT) October 12, 2026 (Mon): Acceptance decisions communicated to authors, on a rolling basis, no later than this date November 16, 2026 (Mon): Regular registration deadline (at least one author must be registered) November 16, 2026 (Mon): Camera-ready submissions due December 12–13, 2026 (Sat/Sun): 2026 Pre-ICIS SIGDSA Symposium For submission guidelines, track details, and registration information, please visit: https://preicis.sigdsa.org/. SIGDSA has received AIS's outstanding SIG award for ten consecutive years, thanks to the wide variety of activities and events offered to members. Learn more about SIGDSA and join at: https://communities.aisnet.org/sigdsa/home. We look forward to welcoming you to Lisbon in December 2026! Conference Co-Chairs Amir Zadeh — amir.zadeh@wright.edu (Wright State University, USA) Leo Vijayasarathy — Leo.Vijayasarathy@colostate.edu (Colorado State University, USA) Local Conference Co-Chair Tiago Oliveira — toliveira@novaims.unl.pt (NOVA Information Management School, Portugal) Program Co-Chairs Zhilei Qiao (George) — qiaozl@uab.edu (University of Alabama at Birmingham, USA) Stephen McCarthy — stephen.mccarthy@ucc.ie (University College Cork, Ireland) On behalf of the 2026 Pre-ICIS SIGDSA Symposium Organizing Committee Please repost and share with colleagues, students, and collaborators who may be interested in decision support, analytics, data science, and AI-enabled decision-making. We look forward to receiving your submissions!

messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues: Episode 108 of the Cybersecurity Readiness Podcast is live, and it tackles a governance blind spot most boards have never once discussed: foundational network infrastructure — DNS, DHCP, and IP address management. My guest is Scott Harrell, President and CEO of Infoblox and former leader of Cisco's $20 billion enterprise networking business . We open with the October 2025 AWS outage — a single misconfiguration in a core routing service that cascaded into an estimated $581 million in losses. It wasn't a sophisticated attack. It was a governance decision nobody had made, because nobody was actively watching the layer [...]

messages.key: digital sovereignty online paper development workshop for special issue in the information systems journal
messages.subject: Digital Sovereignty: Online Paper Development Workshop for Special Issue in the Information Systems Journal
messages.snippet: Call for Participation: Online Paper Development Workshop on Digital Sovereignty We are delighted to announce that we are arranging an online Paper Development Workshop (PDW) for our Information Systems Journal Special Issue on Digital Sovereignty. The aim of the PDW is to give prospective authors for the special issue the opportunity to meet the editors and present and discuss their preliminary work on the topic. To allow authors from different geographic regions of AIS to participate, the workshop will have three different sessions. Digital technologies, digital infrastructures, and platform ecosystems have become critical resources for nations, organisations, communities, and individuals. As a result, questions of who controls digital infrastructures, who governs data, and who sets the rules for digital innovation have become concerns for public administration, economic strategy, and everyday life. Artificial intelligence (AI) is perhaps the domain where the question of digital sovereignty is currently most acute — raising issues of dependence, available alternatives, and open-source solutions. Yet information systems research should have much to contribute, given its socio-technical approach to the study of digital technologies in context. There is a substantial body of information systems research relevant to the digital sovereignty discourse by addressing questions including how states and organisations can navigate global digital ecosystems, how digital architectures and governance standards can be designed to support digital sovereignty, how digital sovereignty influences digital innovation and the short- and long-term implications for individuals' and communities’ autonomy, rights, resilience and risks. In this special issue, we invite empirically grounded and theoretically ambitious research that explains digital sovereignty as a socio-technical accomplishment, including its tensions, trade-offs, and unintended consequences. We also welcome conceptual or review papers on the topic. We expect extended abstracts of studies based on qualitative research, but we also welcome ideas for quantitative contributions, provided they emphasise the research context and the theoretical and practical implications of their findings. Workshop details: Submission deadline: 6th September 2026 (11.59 pm UTC) Notification of acceptance: 18th September 2026 Updated information about the workshop:  https://www.mn.uio.no/ifi/english/research/groups/is/call-for-papers/online-paper-development-workshop-ds/index.html Session’s time and date: Asia-Pacific: September 24th 11:30 PM to 3:30 PM SGT Africa/Europe: September 24th 12:00 PM to 4 PM UTC Americas: September 28th 11:00 AM to 3 PM EDT Format: Online workshop with presentation and interaction with special issue editors Abstract format: Participation is based on an accepted extended abstract of up to 3 pages. The abstract must indicate a plan for a substantial information systems contribution foregrounding digital technologies in context, emphasising the specific nature of digital technologies, infrastructures, and platforms and their social implications Submission instructions Extended abstract of a maximum of 3 pages Email your submission to Coordinating Senior Editor: pnielsen@ifi.uio.no Indicate in the email the session of preference Indicate in the email who will present the work Special issue Submission deadline for full papers: March 31st 2027 Call for papers: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/page/journal/13652575/homepage/call-for-papers/si-2026-000456 Extended call for papers: https://www.mn.uio.no/ifi/english/research/groups/is/special-issue-on-digital-sovereignty.html Contact/questions: Petter Nielsen at pnielsen@ifi.uio.no For questions regarding this workshop, get in touch with Coordinating Senior Editor Petter Nielsen We are looking forward to seeing your contributions,  On behalf of the Senior Editors,  Petter Nielsen Daniel J. Veit Laura Brandimarte Juliana Sutanto Mary Tate Ben Eaton

messages.key: aacsb ais and others shared framework on research impact
messages.subject: AACSB, AIS, and others: Shared framework on research impact
messages.snippet: Dear IS colleagues Many of us care deeply about making an impact through our research but recognize that academic incentives often get in the way. I'm writing to update you on a collective effort to address this problem, and to pay thanks to the AACSB and AOM for kicking it off. messages.key: episode 107 compliant but exposed rethinking grc for real security
messages.subject: Episode 107 -- Compliant but Exposed: Rethinking GRC for Real Security
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues: Compliant. Certified. Breached. That's the uncomfortable reality facing too many organizations today — and it's exactly what Episode 107 of the Cybersecurity Readiness Podcast Series confronts head-on. My guest, Richa Kaul, Founder and CEO of Complyance, brings rare clarity to a problem that has plagued enterprise security governance for years: the dangerous conflation of compliance with security. When your GRC platform is full of green indicators, and your auditors just signed off — but your adversaries are already inside — something is fundamentally broken. [...]

messages.key: historic milestone official launch of the ais gcc chapter
messages.subject: Historic Milestone: Official Launch of the AIS GCC Chapter
messages.snippet: Dear Colleagues and Members of the Information Systems Community, It is my great pleasure to announce a historic milestone for the Information Systems (IS) discipline in the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) region. Following the successful GCC Information Systems Symposium, hosted by Carnegie Mellon University in Qatar on 20 November 2025, participants expressed overwhelming support for establishing a formal regional community for Information Systems scholars and practitioners. In our post-symposium survey, 100% of respondents endorsed the creation of an official GCC Chapter of the Association for Information Systems (AIS). [...]
